name: TNT_EXEC / HNF Sequencer Pack
description: Structured LabWindows/CVI pack for TNT_EXEC test-sequencer DLL functions, reporting helpers, PASS/FAIL status management and logging patterns.

categories.name: Bootstrap & test DLL templates
categories.description: Entry points and skeletons used to run or expose TNT_EXEC C test functions.

functions.name: main.c skeleton for TNT_EXEC
functions.description: Minimal standard-C entry point that starts the TNT_EXEC sequencer.
functions.longDescription: Minimal standard-C entry point that starts the TNT_EXEC sequencer.

functions.name: WinMain skeleton for TNT_EXEC
functions.description: Windows-entry-point variant that passes lpszCmdLine to TNT_EXEC.
functions.longDescription: Windows-entry-point variant that passes lpszCmdLine to TNT_EXEC.

functions.name: mandatory TNT test functions
functions.description: Mandatory test functions typically referenced by a .tex sequence. Init and Begin are the usual places for logging setup, serial number capture and production metadata.
functions.longDescription: Mandatory test functions typically referenced by a .tex sequence. Init and Begin are the usual places for logging setup, serial number capture and production metadata.

functions.name: TNT test function with status and error handling
functions.description: Reusable pattern for a C test function compiled into a DLL and called by the sequencer. The reporting call updates PASS/FAIL and logging.
functions.longDescription: Reusable pattern for a C test function compiled into a DLL and called by the sequencer. The reporting call updates PASS/FAIL and logging.

functions.name: exec_Start
functions.description: Start the TNT_EXEC test sequencer with the current command-line context.
functions.longDescription: Starts the TNT_EXEC sequencer. For a normal C main, pass SIMPLE_MAIN, argv and NULL. For WinMain, pass WIN_MAIN, NULL and lpszCmdLine. Command-line options observed in the function panel include /d development mode, /i run Init after load, /r restrict production open, /f fullscreen, and a .tex path.
functions.returnDescription: No return value.

functions.name: exec_Return_Comp
functions.signature: int exec_Return_Comp(short Type, int Bits, void *Expected, void *Received, char *Message, char *Log_Label, short *Success);
functions.description: Compare expected and received discrete values, report expected/received data, and update PASS/FAIL.
functions.longDescription: Supports string, signed decimal, ASCII, hexadecimal and hex-string comparisons. The returned Success is TEST_PASS or TEST_FAIL; the function also produces report/log output.
functions.returnDescription: 0 when no TNT_EXEC error occurred; non-zero/negative TNT error code otherwise.

options.label: 1 — String
options.description: Expected and received are char*. Bits=0 compares whole strings case-insensitively and ignores whitespace.

options.label: 2 — Decimal
options.description: Expected and received are signed short/long values. Bits > 16 selects long.

options.label: 3 — ASCII
options.description: Expected and received point to one character. Bits is ignored.

options.label: 4 — Hexa
options.description: Expected and received are unsigned short/long values. Bits selects the number of LSB bits compared.

options.label: 5 — Hex String
options.description: Expected and received are char* buffers displayed as hexadecimal bytes. Bits is a byte count.

functions.name: exec_Return_Meas
functions.signature: int exec_Return_Meas(short Type, double Value, short Digits, double Min, double Max, char *Unit, char *Message, char *Log_Label, short *Success);
functions.description: Return a numeric measurement with limits, unit, PASS/FAIL computation and logging.
functions.longDescription: The function rounds Value according to Digits, checks it against the selected limit mode, writes the measurement to the report/log and updates Success.
functions.returnDescription: 0 when no TNT_EXEC error occurred; non-zero/negative TNT error code otherwise.

options.label: 1 — In range
options.description: Passes when Min <= Value <= Max.

options.label: 2 — Greater than
options.description: Passes when Value >= Min. Max is mainly kept for API symmetry.

options.label: 3 — Lesser than
options.description: Passes when Value <= Max. Min is mainly kept for API symmetry.

options.label: 4 — Out of range
options.description: Passes when Value is outside [Min, Max].

items.description: Disable the option or request a read/get operation when the API uses a Set flag.

items.label: 1 — enabled / set
items.description: Enable the option or request a write/set operation when the API uses a Set flag.

functions.name: TEST_* result constants
functions.insertText:
#define TEST_FAIL       0
#define TEST_PASS       1

#define TEST_FAIL_NOLOG 4
